title: cuphea corisperma
intro: Cuphea corisperma, also known as Mexican heather or Mexican plume poppy, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Cuphea corisperma include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce anxiety. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, or it can be made into a tincture for easier consumption.
